PIONEERS MEMORIAL SKILLED NURSING CENTER

CCN: 555557 · BRAWLEY, CA 92227 · Imperial County

Overview

Address
320 CATTLE CALL DR., BRAWLEY, CA 92227

Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1993-06-15
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.43 3.70 4.61 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.25 0.65 0.26 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.30 0.82 1.35 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.87 2.23 3.00 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.55 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.13 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.17
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.31
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.35
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
37.8%
annual
RN turnover
60.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.31
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-10 15 13 2 56 1 56
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-02-20 33 27 6 244 2 366
Total weighted health score
133.5
lower is better

Deficiencies (60)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

6 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
52 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-04-28 2025-05-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-04-28 2025-05-16
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0911 Ensure resident rooms hold no more than 4 residents; for new construction after November 28, 2016, rooms hold no more than 2 residents. B 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2025-01-10 2025-02-14
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-12-18 2025-01-15
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-10-11 2024-11-11
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-25
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. F 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0710 Obtain a doctor's order to admit a resident and ensure the resident is under a doctor's care. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2024-02-20 2024-05-06
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0837 Establish a governing body that is legally responsible for establishing and implementing policies for managing and operating the facility and appoints a properly licensed administrator responsible for managing the facility. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0841 Designate a physician to serve as medical director responsible for implementation of resident care policies and coordination of medical care in the facility. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. F 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly F 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0887 Educate residents and staff on COVID-19 vaccination, offer the COVID-19 vaccine to eligible residents and staff after education, and properly document each resident and staff member's vaccination status. E 2024-02-20 2024-04-11
0911 Ensure resident rooms hold no more than 4 residents; for new construction after November 28, 2016, rooms hold no more than 2 residents. B 2024-02-20 —
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2024-02-20 —
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2023-08-03 2023-08-24
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-08-03 2023-08-24
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-04-12 2023-05-10
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. E 2023-04-12 2023-05-10

Deficiencies 0755 — description Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. 0755.description
Deficiencies 0755 — scope_severity D 0755.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0755 — survey_date 2025-01-10 0755.survey_date
Deficiencies 0755 — tag 0755 0755.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2024-05-06 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity E 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — survey_date 2024-02-20 0758.survey_date
Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0759 — correction_date 2025-02-14 0759.correction_date
Deficiencies 0759 — description Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. 0759.description
Deficiencies 0759 — scope_severity D 0759.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0759 — survey_date 2025-01-10 0759.survey_date
Deficiencies 0759 — tag 0759 0759.tag
Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2024-04-11 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity D 0761.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0761 — survey_date 2024-02-20 0761.survey_date
Deficiencies 0761 — tag 0761 0761.tag

Deficiencies 0806 — description Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. 0806.description
